articles.title: Fish in Danger: Protecting Migratory Fish
articles.summary: Fish that travel long distances in rivers and lakes are in big trouble. Their numbers have dropped by 81% in the last 50 years. This is because of dams, pollution, and too much fishing. These fish cannot reach their breeding places. The United Nations (UN) made a new report to help protect them. They picked 30 special fish, like the golden mahseer and goonch catfish, to save first. These fish are important for people because they provide food and are part of culture. For example, the golden mahseer is called the tiger of the river in the Himalayas. Scientists say we need to stop building dams and overfishing to help these fish survive.

articles.title: How Stars Change as They Grow Old
articles.summary: Scientists used supercomputers to solve a big mystery about giant stars. Red giant stars are old stars that get very big. For 50 years, people wondered why the stuff on the surface of these stars changes. Now, researchers found that the stars' spinning is the key. When stars spin, it helps move material from deep inside to the surface. This explains why we see different chemicals on the surface. The study was done by scientists from universities in Canada and the U.S. They used powerful computers to run 3D simulations, which are like video games that show what happens inside stars. This discovery helps us understand how stars, including our Sun, will change in the future.

articles.title: A New Pill for Diabetes: No More Shots!
articles.summary: Scientists have been trying for over 100 years to make insulin pills for people with diabetes, but it was hard because the body breaks down insulin in the stomach before it can help. Now, a team at Kumamoto University, led by Associate Professor Shingo Ito, has found a way using a special peptide called DNP peptide. This peptide helps insulin pass through the small intestine so it can get into the blood. They used two methods to help insulin cross the intestine barrier. This new pill needs much lower doses than before, making it more practical. It works about 33-41% as well as injections. The researchers hope this pill will replace daily injections, which can be tough for patients. They are doing more tests on animals and models that act like human intestines to get ready for use in people. The study was published in Molecular Pharmaceutics.

articles.title: Scientists Make Glass a Super Tool for Quantum Security
articles.summary: Scientists have found a cool way to use glass to make super-secure communication devices for the future. As quantum computers get stronger, they might break current codes, so we need new methods like quantum cryptography that use light to keep data safe. Researchers from Italy made a tiny device inside borosilicate glass using a special laser. This device helps read quantum signals in light with low loss and works well with fiber-optic systems we already have. Glass is better than silicon because it doesn't care about light polarization and is very stable. The device can do two jobs: create random numbers for security and send secret keys for communication, setting records for speed. This makes quantum technology more practical for real-world use.

articles.title: Super Light Traps for Tiny Materials
articles.summary: Scientists have made a new light trap to help very thin materials shine brighter. These materials, called atom-thin semiconductors, are only one layer of atoms thick and can create light in special ways. But because they are so thin, they don't interact with light much, making their light weak. In a study, researchers placed a thin material called tungsten disulfide on tiny air holes carved into a crystal. These air holes, called Mie voids, trap light inside them, making the light stronger right where the thin material sits. This helps the material emit more light and work better for things like sensors and tiny light sources. The design is different from old traps that use solid materials, and it works well even if the material absorbs light. By adjusting the size of the holes, scientists can control the light color and make the material shine up to 20 times brighter. This new method could lead to better technology for computers and communication.

articles.title: US Pays Company to Stop Wind Energy
articles.summary: The US government is giving $1 billion to a company called TotalEnergies to stop building wind farms in the ocean. This company had plans to make clean energy from wind, but now it will use the money to work on oil and gas projects instead. The deal means TotalEnergies won't try to build any more offshore wind farms in the US. Some people think this is strange because wind energy can help lower costs for families, but the government says it will make energy cheaper. The company had two big projects, including one that could power many homes. This decision might make it harder for states to get clean energy.
